name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Proud
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
I want to thank all of you who made a contribution or volunteered at the close of such an important quarter. This is an amazing campaign of people, and I am so proud of all of you across this country and of what we’ve achieved together so far.
#
I want to thank all of you who made a contribution or volunteered at the close of such an important quarter. This is an amazing campaign of people, and I am so proud of all of you across this country and of what we’ve achieved together so far.
Because of you, we’ve overcome each obstacle – including the critical fundraising goal we set. We’re proving what’s possible without a dime from a single PAC, when we shut the door on lobbyists, corporations, and special interests. We’re showing what we can do when people come together from all walks of life, damning the differences, and deciding that what unites us is far stronger than what divides us.
As Amy and I prepare to return to Iowa – this time with Ulysses, Molly, and Henry for their first trip – I’m thinking of your stories and the many reasons you are part of this.
[Will you let us know what inspired you to contribute to our campaign?](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/share-your-story/?source=ema20190701-39953&t=1&akid=39953%2E444211%2EXQ3jJd) I want to share your stories with our campaign staff and volunteers. They mean so much to us, and they power us through any tough moment.
I’m also thinking of the kids and families I met yesterday morning when I walked across the Paso del Norte bridge and into Mexico – people who came to our country seeking asylum, but who have been turned back by this administration's unlawful and inhumane policies.
It’s on every single one of us to make things right. We must end the Remain in Mexico policy, end metering, end family separation, and end the detention of those who pose absolutely no threat. But we must go far beyond that and fix the entire system.
So I want to thank not just people who volunteered or contributed to our campaign – but all of you who turned out in the heat this weekend to protest the cruel treatment of children and the separation of families, and all of you who donated to the Diocesan Migrant & Refugee Services when we put out the call for help.
While our greatest challenges are still ahead, what we’ve done so far reminds me that together, we have the courage and the confidence to meet each of them.
[I hope you’ll share your story about what inspired you to contribute so we can share it with our team. In a campaign powered entirely by people, it’s you and your stories that keep us going.](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/share-your-story/?source=ema20190701-39953&t=2&akid=39953%2E444211%2EXQ3jJd)
I’m honored to be running this race with you. Thanks for being part of this.
Beto
P.S. If you’re able to donate now or missed the deadline, every contribution is still a big help – especially as we turn to our work organizing in Iowa and setting our sights on what’s next to continue building this historic campaign.
**[Contribute](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/18062?refcode=ema20190701-39953&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=39953%2E444211%2EXQ3jJd)**

Paid for by Beto for America
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This campaign is powered by grassroots supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=39953%2E444211%2EXQ3jJd). To contribute via check, please address to Beto for America campaign, P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923.

name: Gov. Mike Huckabee
subject: These debates are like a game show!
body:
| | Peter, Twenty candidates participated in the Democratic Presidential Debates last week - and it was political entertainment at its finest. Let me say, as someone who has personally been through two cycles of presidential debates - there is nothing that resembles a debate in these forums - whether it’s Democrat or Republican. These debates are like a game show. It’s absurd that a debate anchor will pontificate for minutes presenting a question then say the candidate has 30 seconds to answer. They are not designed to inform the voters, and the respective parties, about the candidates, their qualifications, or their positions on issues that matter to you, the voter. Enough said. ****[Now we must inform Christian voters from a Christian perspective; we can’t rely on national TV networks sound bites. We need your help to reach faith voters _before_ 2020](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p190702apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190702apl01nd&n=1).**** **Critical moments from the debates pulled back the curtain to reveal the left are denying common sense and principles of truth at an unprecedented level.** Candidates were all vying to see who could pander the greatest and promise the most free stuff to the most people (free college, free health care, free abortions!) For example, Julian Castro not only wants taxpayers to pay for abortions, he wants us to pay for abortions for men who identify as women. Try to figure that one out. Elizabeth Warren’s solution to illegal immigration is simply to make it legal to enter the country illegally. Pete Buttigieg scolded the Republican party for associating itself with Christianity, that it “has lost all claim to ever use religious language again.” And all the statements in favor of socialist policies should send a chill down our spines. If you are disheartened by the direction these candidates want to take our nation - I want to ask you to make your best donation to My Faith Votes today. **[Your gift of $5, $15, or $25 - will make a difference in our effort](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p190702apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190702apl01nd&n=2).** At My Faith Votes, we’re elevating family values, liberty and biblical principles over an invented view of gender, family, and a love for socialism. One thing is clear; we can’t rely on the debates to shape people’s opinions. Thirty-second answers promising free stuff, frankly, lies to people about basic economics and freedoms. This is why we must reach 90 million Christians in America to know the truth and vote. **If we hope to activate the most powerful voting block for biblical values, we have to kick it into high gear right now.** **[Please make your best gift to My Faith Votes today to protect life, religious freedom, and marriage and family in 2020](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p190702apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190702apl01nd&n=3).** We have a ground game strategy mobilizing Christians in key districts and states across the nation, and a national campaign to ensure every Christian is prepared to vote for biblical values. Thank you for your commitment to stand boldly to see American ideals preserved for the next generation. I hope I can count on you. [](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p190702apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190702apl01nd&n=4) **United. We Stand.**

Gov. Mike Huckabee
Honorary National Chairman
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190702apl01nd&n=5)
---

Thanks so much for joining Team Warner, Derek!
We're so glad to know that you're standing with Mark as he fights in the Senate to protect our healthcare, preserve the integrity of our democracy, and ensure our economy works for everyone. **This campaign is a grassroots effort** , so your involvement is critical to our success.
Right now, your support is more important than ever: **National Republicans have already named Mark as a top target for 2020, and President Trump personally told Virginia Republicans that he believes Virginia is in play!** But we know Mark has something going for him that's even stronger than the dark money and attack ads Republicans may have on their side -- and that's this grassroots team.
We're going to keep you in the loop with periodic email updates about the issues Mark is working on and how you can help, but we also want to make it as easy as possible for you to stay informed. **So in the meantime, will you sign up for Mark 's updates on social media?**

**[Like Mark on Facebook](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d258/53d9897e/51f78c2e/1c8f8a28/3747941488/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/MarkWarnerVa/")**

**[Follow Mark on Twitter](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d258/53d9897e/51f78c2e/1c8f8a17/3747941488/VEsF/ "https://twitter.com/markwarnerva")**

subject: Celebrate Independence Day With Us!
body:
**
Good news Peter!
------------------------------------------------------------

Last week, we asked for your help to reach our goal at the end of the quarter. I couldn't be more proud and grateful for your support in getting us to where we need to be.

With the start of a new quarter, we're going to need your help to keep up the pace, and stay on top! Be sure to donate (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/marcflores?utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=fdd5cdc86e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2019_07_01_07_11&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-fdd5cdc86e-32267927) today (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/marcflores?utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=fdd5cdc86e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2019_07_01_07_11&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-fdd5cdc86e-32267927) .

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/marcflores?utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=fdd5cdc86e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2019_07_01_07_11&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-fdd5cdc86e-32267927

Celebrate the 4th With Us

I'd like to personally invite you to join myself and my campaign staff for a 4th of July Barbecue! The event will take place from 12-3 at my house. Come on out to enjoy some great food, lively conversations, and patriotic celebrations! name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: How Team Trump is Keeping Drug Prices Down
body:
Most Americans like their health insurance—especially the 240 million on either Medicare or an employer-sponsored plan | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7c9254e282&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **How Team Trump is Keeping Drug Prices Down**

Most Americans like their health insurance—especially the 240 million on either Medicare or an employer-sponsored plan. Rather than kicking millions of Americans off their insurance with Medicare for All, President Donald J. Trump is reforming the healthcare system to keep costs down while protecting patient choice.

“At some point, almost all Americans have been stuck with massive, unexpected medical bills or forced to make health decisions without real information or anyone to guide them,” HHS Secretary Alex Azar and White House Domestic Policy Council Director Joe Grogan write in the _New York Post_. “It’s one reason why President Trump signed an executive order last week to help you easily find the typical price — and what you would actually owe — for major health services before you have to purchase them,” they add.

“President Trump has promised a better vision: a health care system that treats you like a person, not a number. He wants to hold providers and Big Pharma accountable.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=60feac046d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “President Trump has now gotten the US economic expansion to outlast any other in modern history,” the [_New York Post_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8df96f1d82&e=3ee4985eea) editorial board writes. By historical standards, even with the weak growth of the Obama era, the economy was overdue for a dip when Trump took office. “Yet it has instead remained pretty strong . . . Trump’s deregulation and tax cuts, each a stark reverse of Obama policies, changed the course.”
---
|
---
| “The Trump administration’s Peace to Prosperity plan for the West Bank and Gaza is sensible and offers the best hope for building sustainable economies in the region,” Columbia Business School economist Glenn Hubbard writes in [_The Wall Street Journal_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=de6f924bf1&e=3ee4985eea). If implemented, it would marshal $50 billion in investment over 10 years for the Palestinian people and neighboring Arab countries. “The White House’s new plan is based on two important economic observations. First, private business, not charity, is what drives prosperity. Second, Palestinian businesses need access to capital.”
---
|
---
| “By the left’s account you’d think the Trump Administration’s only ambition on health care is to rip insurance from the poor and sick. So note that a Health and Human Services rule finalized last month represents a dramatic expansion in health-care choices for those who may have limited insurance options,” [_The Wall Street Journal_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=97b5eab05c&e=3ee4985eea) editorial board writes. The new rule will allow employers to give workers tax-exempt dollars for purchasing health insurance in the individual market—another option the Obama Administration tried to limit through the Affordable Care Act.
---
|
---
| “The time is long overdue to usher in a new trade agreement with our North American neighbors. American businesses and workers have been forced to operate under a 20th century trade deal unfit for our 21st century economy,” Rep. Brad Wenstrup (R-OH) writes in [_The Columbus Dispatch_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c5f01cd198&e=3ee4985eea). “Delaying consideration of USMCA tells our trading partners that we’re not serious about doing business with them.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=135e45aef2&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2c5b8a2c32&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=bd1192154a)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

name: Dan Baer
subject: Thank you!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan for Colorado
body:
We're living through history here - this is a moment. But it is a moment that is only possible because of people like you.
|
---

| | Hello!

I want to say THANK YOU - I know you've been getting a lot of emails from us recently, but I wanted to share why and share some great news with you as well!

**We just announced that, because of your help, we raised over $1 million in our first fundraising quarter. (And by the way, we set a record for the largest-ever first quarter fundraising total for an LGBTQ+ candidate for U.S. Senate or U.S. House!)** It's a strong first step on the long road to defeat Senator Cory Gardner, flip the Senate and oust Mitch McConnell. | [](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82672/232dfd1/9894dca/62a10503/3159046358/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=20190702_em_fr_b1_baer_all&amount=19&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")
---

**On the first day of this campaign, I said I believe it is never only one person who rises to meet a moment. People across Colorado and from all 50 states throughout this country are rising to meet this moment with us.**

People I've met want to restore dignity and honor to our politics--they are fed up with career politicians and are ready for fresh voices. The people I meet are eager to find public servants who are running for office because they want to put in the work and do the job. And the people I talk with still care--they believe that we can make progress, indeed that we must do so, by working together for a politics that delivers.

[](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82672/232dfd1/9894dca/62a10503/3159046358/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=20190702_em_fr_b1_baer_all&amount=19&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")
---

**We 're living through history here - this is a moment. But it is a moment that is only possible because of people like you.**

People who have raised their hands to reset our politics. People who have stood up and said "I care." People who have declared loudly and with one voice that they reject cynicism and instead embrace the big ideas required to tackle the challenges facing our country.

I'm only able to run because I stand on the shoulders of others - those who have fought for progress long before me, and those who are fighting as we speak.

So where do we go from here?

We're absolutely going to keep this momentum going - there are always more mountains to climb, so please follow our campaign on [Facebook](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82672/232dfd1/9894dca/62a10500/3159046358/VEsF/ "http://www.facebook.com/DanForColorado/"), [Twitter,](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82672/232dfd1/9894dca/62a10501/3159046358/VEsC/ "https://twitter.com/danbbaer") and [Instagram](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82672/232dfd1/9894dca/62a10506/3159046358/VEsD/ "http://www.instagram.com/danforcolorado/") to keep in touch!

But at the same time, it is important to stop and appreciate what we have accomplished together during moments like these.

So, from everyone here at the campaign, from Brian and from me: thank you. We're just getting started, but we've started strong, and it's all because of you.

Thanks again,

\--Dan

body:

| |
---
| | I'm speechless.

I can't believe that I stood on that debate stage thanks to you.

I can't believe that I got to share my unique story and speak for communities like mine.

I hope I made you proud.

I'm counting on you for one last favor:**_[Your support has gotten me 85% of the way to qualifying for the next Democratic debate. Before midnight, can you pitch in so we can prove to everyone that our campaign has the momentum to win? >>](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcdbd/3eb3e2fc/46456820/744ac8be/2047446026/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/julian-em?refcode=20190702_em_fr_b1_castro_all_rs&recur_weekly=on&recurring=on&amount=25")_**

I'll tell you what I told Anderson Cooper:

**I know I can win this race.**

I know we can win because when voters hear our message, they support me.

(That's why Nate Silver himself said voters are 7 times more likely to vote for me after the first debate!)

But make no mistake: The media and the establishment are looking for any excuse to count us out.

That's why this moment is so important.

I need to know I have your support so we can qualify for the next debates and prove wrong everyone who said a kid from the West Side of San Antonio doesn't have a shot.

I'm humbly asking: Can you chip in $19 before midnight?

subject: It means an awful lot
body:

I'd like to quickly say thank you for your incredible support these past few months. You've all shown me how strongly you believe in our campaign for a better Mississippi, and that means an awful lot.
Hi friend, I'd like to quickly say **thank you** for your incredible support these past few months. You've all shown me how strongly you believe in our campaign for a better Mississippi, and that means an awful lot.

My campaign for governor is about _you_. We're all Mississippians, so it doesn't matter if you're a Republican or a Democrat. We all want health care that we can afford, quality education for our kids, fair pay for teachers, and a strong economy that lifts us all up.

Thanks to supporters like you, my team and I will be able to talk to more folks, knock on more doors, and spread our vision for how we can move forward -- together. And after we win in November, I know we can make that vision a reality for all Mississippians.

Thank you again.

\-- Jim Hood

body:
What the U.S. is Doing to Migrant Children is an Unspeakable Disgrace [ ](http://links.johnkasich.com/u/click?_t=6a3b28686d0a4d77958e12349305a4fe&_m=57f56111891b4257990839b55b0ee9a1&_e=n8K-bv7VuVktsDKbc3rVXtolMzSu1BBqozMPh58mlHPbEFdh7rKjxYc8vvdRKVucGrzCh3qZa-4M-gd7_qvAf0vpTcc5jINYyZ-8KeBwoUumNbKG-hDYmsjQMjuztndMQGUBfd4yLEQP7w-O0UCtzcAsJuVJbjrBksVj6kwjbxRbFFr9pBoNE_UQ3FI3yj64heyzKu2yZ-9ImYJIFK6653vZVXw0226PpWugsW16CM5naYHeAovYNHs0ZGamMOBO_HRqLkf_ReWDdVjVkxP6pw%3D%3D) | |

**Governor John Kasich's CNN Op-Ed:**
**[What the U.S. is Doing to Migrant Children is an Unspeakable Disgrace](http://links.johnkasich.com/u/click?_t=6a3b28686d0a4d77958e12349305a4fe&_m=57f56111891b4257990839b55b0ee9a1&_e=n8K-bv7VuVktsDKbc3rVXiK35QRVNK3vOJy05M7YGng7QBlPGK3VuUHeAWtsAaBACbqUgcnz9OqBCs3pXN1xx1GjGaxmRSDqxPEVddcFT0Ehabe6ecMH3m_Xw0Udy3F8JiAX01X7WbkDnyZIBtJLk-PQR3tE6ItVxLXwbDgHNdauPncGOKXIYIptengR4umqdbgdEm19V77KxrwYG38emmLk7fLtX8N_ImS0ojuOb2FdEnkILIpatU8R9I_50PChGwSwdaJOk7i0lGXk5n3I4ub1poKXl7dx0pAhFJ6S0ZASKREZDMijsp7Jl1bM-DbJySIkmwV4q8PeGKgw-3hvQ8WzqYwuYdaV0XSIthorbKU%3D)**
America's war on children must stop.
What's happening on our southern border -- migrant children in overcrowded, unsanitary and unsafe conditions, separated from their families -- is an unspeakable disgrace and a stain on this nation we may never wash away.
Watching and reading reports about the gross neglect and mistreatment we're inflicting on kids is appalling. I ask myself, "Can this be America? Is this the way we in this country treat any human being, let alone the youngest, most fragile and most innocent among us?" But it's true. What we're seeing now is undeniably un-American, inhumane and shameful.
For no offense of their own, sick and hungry children are left to sleep on concrete, in their own filth, without adequate nutrition and medical care. Infants and toddlers, separated from their families, are being cared for by other youngsters who may be only a few years older. Is this happening because of cruel intent, scandalous neglect or a lack of funding? Ultimately, I care less about the answer to those questions and whom we can blame, and more about how we can help these children.
Blame and dissension will not bring us solutions. That will take leadership. Republicans and Democrats in Congress need to set aside their differences and drop the endless back-and-forth finger-pointing, as well as the fighting within their own camps. Most of all, the President of United States has to be the leader here. There is no more urgent moral issue before him, but he's been engaged in the partisan blame game. That's unacceptable.
Ending this war on children has become an afterthought in Washington's endless struggle over immigration policy. But this crisis at our border is more urgent. These are children. This is America. And we have to put the children first! Without delay, marshal this nation's resources to provide safe, humane conditions for children being held, while moving just as quickly to get as many as possible out of detention and into the care of family members or other sponsors.
This means pushing our current fixation on election debates, town halls and other political concerns to one side -- and doing it right now -- so we can take care of these children first. Immigration policies, long-term fixes and the inevitable politics can come later.
Do we need secure borders? Of course. Do we need a total overhaul of our system of legal immigration? Absolutely. Republicans and Democrats should work together to pass immigration reform that meets our economic and security needs while also creating jobs for Americans. But put the needs of these children first.
In recent days there have been hopeful signs. The House and Senate have moved forward quickly on supplemental funding to ease conditions at the border. But Congress and the President need to come to a compromise that ensures the safety and well-being of these children. They must also realize that funding alone is not enough.
A real solution will require much greater accountability, a real plan for going forward and a bipartisan commitment to taking care of these kids. All of this will take time, but the needs of these children will not wait. Today -- here and now — this business of crowding children into jail-like detention centers, while denying them adequate nutrition, sanitation and medical care has to stop. No debate, no excuses, no finger-pointing. Just stop!
This disgraceful disregard for our own values is not who we are as Americans. It's not who we have ever been. To ourselves, and to so many others throughout the world, our country has represented the promise of something better. At some point we must step back, come together and do what is right for these kids. Because, only then, will our actions be right for America.

Ending this war on children has become an afterthought in Washington's endless struggle over immigration policy.
|

For no offense of their own, sick and hungry children are left to sleep on concrete, in their own filth, without adequate nutrition and medical care. Infants and toddlers, separated from their families, are being cared for by other youngsters who may be only a few years older. Is this happening because of cruel intent, scandalous neglect or a lack of funding? Ultimately, I care less about the answer to those questions and whom we can blame, and more about how we can help these children. Blame and dissension will not bring us solutions. That will take leadership. Republicans and Democrats in Congress need to set aside their differences and drop the endless back-and-forth finger-pointing, as well as the fighting within their own camps. Most of all, the President of United States has to be the leader here. There is no more urgent moral issue before him, but he's been engaged in the partisan blame game. That's unacceptable. Ending this war on children has become an afterthought in Washington's endless struggle over immigration policy. But this crisis at our border is more urgent. These are children. This is America. And we have to put the children first! Without delay, marshal this nation's resources to provide safe, humane conditions for children being held, while moving just as quickly to get as many as possible out of detention and into the care of family members or other sponsors. This means pushing our current fixation on election debates, town halls and other political concerns to one side -- and doing it right now -- so we can take care of these children first. Immigration policies, long-term fixes and the inevitable politics can come later. Do we need secure borders? Of course. Do we need a total overhaul of our system of legal immigration? Absolutely. Republicans and Democrats should work together to pass immigration reform that meets our economic and security needs while also creating jobs for Americans. But put the needs of these children first. In recent days there have been hopeful signs. The House and Senate have moved forward quickly on supplemental funding to ease conditions at the border. But Congress and the President need to come to a compromise that ensures the safety and well-being of these children. They must also realize that funding alone is not enough. A real solution will require much greater accountability, a real plan for going forward and a bipartisan commitment to taking care of these kids. All of this will take time, but the needs of these children will not wait. Today -- here and now — this business of crowding children into jail-like detention centers, while denying them adequate nutrition, sanitation and medical care has to stop. No debate, no excuses, no finger-pointing. Just stop! This disgraceful disregard for our own values is not who we are as Americans. It's not who we have ever been. To ourselves, and to so many others throughout the world, our country has represented the promise of something better. At some point we must step back, come together and do what is right for these kids. Because, only then, will our actions be right for America. Ending this war on children has become an afterthought in Washington's endless struggle over immigration policy. | Share This: | | ---|--- Paid for by Kasich for America, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.www.JohnKasich.com

| | Tonight -- live on Rachel Maddow -- I'll discuss my immigration plan, which drove the conversation in the Democratic debates. [_**I hope you 'll take a moment to read about it -- and join me and the other top Democratic candidates in supporting it. >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcdbe/3eb3e2fc/4660ba7e/744ad70f/775324778/VEsE/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/immigration-survey?source=20190702_em_pet_b2_castro_all_rs") My grandma came to America when she was just seven years old after being separated from her dying mother. I watched the pain of never getting to say goodbye haunt her even as a seventy-year-old woman. I see her image in the children at our borders today -- children as young as infants ripped from their parents, locked in cages, and even "lost" in the system. My grandmother's memory -- and this president's defining failure -- has led me to this decision: [_**I wanted to tell you personally about my first groundbreaking policy proposal -- People First Immigration. I 'm asking you to sign your name and show your support. >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcdbe/3eb3e2fc/4660ba7e/744ad70f/775324778/VEsF/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/immigration-survey?source=20190702_em_pet_b2_castro_all_rs") I won't lie to you: My proposal is bold. But it's what our country needs. People First Immigration centers around 3 key principles: -No more family separation or children in cages -- period. -Bring DREAMers and other undocumented folks out of the shadows -- full stop. -Address the root causes of migrant crises -- this is key. I promised you that I was the complete antithesis to Donald Trump. And People First Immigration makes good on that promise. [_**I need 50,000 signatures-strong before midnight to show the world that America demands an immigration system that restores our faith in people, and puts hatred and bigotry in our rear view mirror. Will you add your name? >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcdbe/3eb3e2fc/4660ba7e/744ad70f/775324778/VEsC/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/immigration-survey?source=20190702_em_pet_b2_castro_all_rs") Talk soon, Julian P.S. Make sure to watch me live on Maddow tonight at 9pm ET/8pm CT!

name: Wilmot Collins
subject: A note from the campaign trail (Montana Senate)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by A whole lot of folks for Wilmot Collins, PO Box 458, Helena, MT 59624
body:
#
Team,
I want to update you on our past week on the campaign trail:
Last Sunday, I attended the first official meeting of the newly re-activated Chouteau County Democrats in Fort Benton, Montana, a town of 1500 people. The chair told us that in the 50 years he's been organizing with the Chouteau County Democrats, this was the biggest crowd he can remember.
We talked about how our tariff and trade policies are hurting the agricultural community by making our agricultural products more expensive in overseas markets. This not only hurts our communities in the short term -- it leads to long-term loss of access to markets in other countries.
At the end of the meeting, I asked supporters to pitch in for gas money to help me keep traveling Montana. After we finished, an elderly gentleman walked up to me and said he was on a fixed income, but talked it over with his wife and agreed more people need to hear our message. He contributed enough to get our campaign to Bozeman.
**[Team, can you follow his example and chip in $5 now to keep gas in our tank and enable me to keep traveling around to talk with Montana voters?](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190702&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**
[****](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190702&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
On Tuesday, I caught up with friends and supporters in Helena. My friend Rachel Caroll Rivas even read a very sweet poem about me as an introduction!
[](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190702&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
On Wednesday, I held a listening session in Missoula. I've held listening sessions like this one throughout my time as Mayor because I believe our elected officials must show up and listen to people about what's going on in their lives in order to help them. The same holds true for our senators -- and so we talked about my vision of taking people's concerns to Washington and changing the way we interact with our representatives in Congress.
[](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190702&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Finally, Wednesday night, I headed to Steve Daines' home town of Bozeman for a candidate "speed dating" event hosted by the Gallatin County Dems. I spoke with over 100 people in small groups of 6-8 people at a time, taking questions and talking about my commitment to being accessible as a public official.
[](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190702&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
**[Team, weeks like these -- weeks of traveling around the state, connecting with Montana voters and spreading our message -- are how we're going to win this campaign. Chip in $5 to fuel our campaign now.](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190702&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**
More updates from the road soon,
Wilmot

**[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.wilmotcollins. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190702&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**

* * *
Paid for by A whole lot of folks for Wilmot Collins, PO Box 458, Helena, MT 59624
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Supporters like you are a crucial part of our team, but if you'd like to unsubscribe, you can [click here](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/56164749/1e3760f0/69d8960f/5f7eac66/2294292529/VEsO/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). To change your email address or update your contact info, [click here.](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/56164749/1e3760f0/69d8960f/5f7eac67/2294292529/VEsP/ "https://wilmotcollins.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/update-your-contact-info?source=footer") [Make a contribution now to elect Wilmot Collins as Montana's next U.S. Senator.](https://act.wilmotcollins.com/page/m/56164749/1e3760f0/69d8960f/5f7eac64/2294292529/VEsHBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=footer")

name: Cory Booker
subject: As president, I'll fight to transform our immigration system from one that tears families and communities apart, to one that treats immigrants with dignity.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
| | |
---
| Friend, Separating children from their families. Detaining human beings in cages. No access to soap or adequate sleep. Shutting the door on refugees seeking asylum. **America, this is not who we are.** Our immigration system is in crisis, and fixing it starts by acknowledging this truth: **When people come to this country, they don 't leave their human rights at the border.** That's why, as president, I'll fight to transform our immigration system from one that tears families and communities apart to one that treats immigrants with dignity. I will virtually eliminate immigrant detention by using executive authority to stop the treatment of immigrants as criminals, close inhumane DHS facilities, end the use of for-profit detention facilities, and get rid of unnecessary barriers for refugees and those seeking asylum. If we want to repair the deep fractures in trust and alleviate the fear that's been instilled in so many communities across our country by the Trump administration, we need to act now. **Donald Trump didn 't wait for Congress to create this crisis, and I will not wait for Congress to solve it.** **Here 's what will happen on Day One of my presidency:**
* Restore and expand protections for DACA, DAPA and TPS recipients.
* Ensure that our system treats immigrants, refugees, and asylum seekers with dignity.
* Modernize our immigration policies and hold ICE and CBP accountable.
* Reaffirm our values by expanding legal pathways to citizenship for immigrants and rolling back the Trump Administration's anti-immigrant policies.
* Reverse President Trump's "zero-tolerance" policy.

That's not all I'll do about immigration on Day One as president (check out a full list [**here**](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc099b/5fca0d19/8e7916f/291bde9d/547101194/VEsH/ "https://corybooker.com/issues/immigration/policy-page/")), but the truth is that's still 567 days away. **We need to step up for the thousands of families suffering under the Trump administration 's cruel, un-American policies. [So please, add your name if you agree that it's time to reform our immigration system.](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc099b/5fca0d19/8e7916f/291bde9c/547101194/VEsE/ "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/immigration-system?source=em_pet_20190702_CB_20190702-pet-CB-immigration-actives_actives_l1")** Together, we can make our immigration system reflect our American values of freedom, justice, and opportunity for all. Thank you, Cory
---
| | Paid for by Cory 2020
---

This campaign is moving quickly. If you want the most up-to-date information, follow Cory on [Instagram](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc099b/5fca0d19/8e7916f/291bde9a/547101194/VEsC/ "https://www.instagram.com/corybooker/"), [Twitter](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc099b/5fca0d19/8e7916f/291bde99/547101194/VEsD/ "https://twitter.com/corybooker"), and [Facebook](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc099b/5fca0d19/8e7916f/291bde98/547101194/VEsA/ "https://www.facebook.com/corybooker").
It's time for leadership that can reignite our spirit of common purpose. That takes all of us. Is this not your preferred email address? You can update your contact info [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc099b/5fca0d19/8e7916f/291bde9b/547101194/VEsO/ "https://corybooker.com/join?source=em_emfooter").

name: Voter Protection Project
subject: 55 yrs after the Civil Rights Act, the GOP is using this loophole to attack voting rights
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Voter Protection Project and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee
body:
---
| | Today we honor a monumental moment in our nation's history: the 55th anniversary of the Civil Rights Act of 1964! This landmark legislation ended segregation in public places and banned employment discrimination on the basis of race, color, religion, sex or national origin- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Peter --
### Today we honor a monumental moment in our nation's history:
## **the 55th anniversary of the Civil Rights Act of 1964**
[](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d072d/72412298/43e31547/61024c84/1253760804/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://go.protectvoting.org/Restore-The-Voting-Rights-Act?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190702_RestoreVotingRightsAct_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190702_RestoreVotingRightsAct_U1_X1") **This landmark legislation ended segregation in public places and banned employment discrimination on the basis of race, color, religion, sex or national origin.** It also laid the foundation for crucial follow-up legislation such as the **Voting Rights Act** barring discriminatory voting practices. **Unfortunately, the Supreme Court dealt a major blow to the legacy of the civil rights movement when it _gutted_ the Voting Rights Act. **The court's decision in the 2013 Shelby County v. Holder case invalidated a key portion of the act requiring states with a history of discriminatory voting laws to have their voting rules approved by the federal government -- allowing racist GOP lawmakers to enact an agenda of voter suppression. subject: President Trump signs $4.6 billion in humanitarian border assistance
body:
The bill provides desperately needed funding for America’s immigration law enforcement officers and includes resources for medical care... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4ad9a7e9c5&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • July 2, 2019
---
| | |
# **President Trump signs $4.6 billion in humanitarian border assistance**

**President Donald J. Trump** [signed a bill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5bf688e5ff&e=3ee4985eea) yesterday evening that will deliver $4.6 billion in emergency funding for humanitarian aid and security at our southern border.

**“For many weeks,** Democrats were giving us a hard time,” the President said from the Oval Office. But in the end, “it became a bipartisan bill. We were very happy about it.”

**The bill provides** desperately needed funding for America’s immigration law enforcement officers and includes resources for medical care, shelters, and increased housing for minors through the Department of Health and Human Services. But the package is only a short-term remedy, not a long-term solution to our border crisis.

**Something to share:** [Border Patrol agents thwart human smuggling attempt](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f47974e61f&e=3ee4985eea)

**“This is a humane solution** to a tremendous problem that’s caused because we have bad immigration laws, and we can solve that problem very, very quickly if we could get together with the Democrats,” President Trump said. “It shouldn’t be this way. We’re the only ones in the world that have a system like this. It’s absolutely insane, our system of immigration.”

**America’s broken asylum laws** have fueled an unprecedented surge at the border that has stretched our immigration facilities to the breaking point. If the current pace holds, more than 1 million illegal immigrants will be apprehended trying to enter the United States this year—more than the population of seven U.S. states.

**Among the biggest** of these loopholes comes from the _Flores_ settlement agreement. Those terms, coupled with additional rulings from activist judges, have forced immigration officials to release alien families into the American interior after only a short period of time in detention. The result is a free pass for anyone arriving at the U.S. border with a minor, leading children to be exploited as a means of beating the system.

**U.S. officials,** including President Trump, have been warning for more than a year about people using abducted or abandoned children to pose as family units and gain entry into the United States—“a claim that critics said was overblown,” Wendy Fry of [_The San Diego Union-Tribune_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e5cfaf45ed&e=3ee4985eea) reports. “But now, authorities in Tijuana are warning migrant mothers to keep their children close by and supervised.”

**“I can’t go to work** because I can’t take my eyes off my boys,” a Honduran mother said. “They want to rob our kids so they can cross into the United States.”

**Although Congressional Democrats** continue to block any legislation addressing these long-term drivers of dangerous illegal immigration, signs of progress are beginning to break through. As of today, nearly three-quarters of Americans say the situation at the southern border with Mexico is a crisis, according to a new CNN poll. “Back in January, just 23% of Democrats called it a crisis as President Donald Trump took that line,” Jennifer Agiesta reports.

**“Now, 70% of Democrats** see the situation at the border as a crisis.”

[In photos: Border Patrol agents save a 13-year-old who was drowning in the Rio Grande.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a2bd6cfb1e&e=3ee4985eea)

**Better late than never:** [Top Democrats Declared Border Crisis ‘Fake’ Over Past Year](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=61c3a3b8a8&e=3ee4985eea)
---

body:
new polling shows that Bernie Sanders is the most popular candidate in the entire Democratic field.
#

Thanks to all of your incredible grassroots support -- the political revolution that we have been working towards is closer to becoming a reality than ever before. When reviewing your responses to our debate survey, it was clear that our movement understands that the crisis our democracy faces requires bold action on many critical issues. Our Revolution places the highest importance on tackling the immediate danger of climate change, but we also understand how important _Medicare for All_ really is.

**Here is a breakdown of results, many of you choosing not one issue, but 6 or 7 of our top 15 issues of today:**
[](https://go.ourrevolution. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-july-fr?amount=27&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=22123&phone=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=ourrev&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190702_1_1&source=OR_EM_FR_90DO_190702_1_1")
**Now, thanks to your tireless organizing to reclaim a democracy that works for the people of America, new polling shows that Bernie Sanders is the most popular candidate in the entire Democratic field.** And no wonder, the ideas we heard on the debate stage -- getting big money out of politics, Medicare for All, free college education, ending private prisons, and more have become mainstream ideas within the Democratic party.

We all know that those ideas didn't become popular because of the corporate owned media. On the contrary, our vision of a democracy of, for, and by the people is continually demonized and slandered.

Despite these attacks from frivolous pundits who treat politics as a sport -- and dangerously naive presidential candidates who spent their first day in the race attending swanky fundraisers and taking money from Health Insurance and Comcast executives -- our ideas are winning, and it wouldn't be possible without you.

[That's why we are taking the time to sincerely thank you for all of your hard work. Together, we've already changed America. Now that we've come so far, we have a responsibility to future generations to finish what we've started.](https://go.ourrevolution.
